seoTitle: Peel vs Pejar — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Peel and Pejar. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Peel and Pejar.
heading: Peel vs Pejar — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Peel, New South Wales and Pejar, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Peel has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Pejar (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Peel or Pejar safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Peel has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Pejar scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Peel scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Pejar scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Peel or Pejar?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Peel has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
